text:
In a small town, there lived a girl named Lucy. Every day after school, she'd wander through the woods by her house. One sunny afternoon, she came across an old treehouse.

Curiosity got the best of her, and she climbed into an empty bedroom, hidden between branches. There was a picture of her family, missing her terribly. She stayed until dusk, painting little stars on the walls. Then, she heard footsteps.

She peeked through the window; two gray owls were there. One was big and wise, fluttering closer. Lucy talked to him.

"I've missed you," she said.

The owl hoot gently. "We've missed you too."

They spent hours chatting. Suddenly, a branch snapped. The big owl scanned the woods.

"I think those hawks are coming!" he whispered.

Lucy prepared her paintbrush.

"Let me protect you," she said.

She painted her feathers bright colors. They flew south.

Years passed, and Lucy
